rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for interface-level assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model when writing AGREE contracts.
generic_rules: Component type contracts should define assumptions and guarantees for port behaviors using visible feature names.
Implementation contracts should bind variables to subcomponent ports and features in valid implementation scope.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the input AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees must reference valid component ports or features in their respective scopes.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes or vice versa.
Do not assume numeric or Boolean encodings for event ports without explicit data values in the model.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
Model analyst extraction of AADL structure
